stories.headline: Trump Calls for Depriving Immigrants Who Illegally Cross Border of Due Process Rights
stories.summary: The president wants to immediately send back immigrants who cross the border illegally without letting them see a judge.

stories.headline: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez Defeats Joseph Crowley in Major Democratic House Upset
stories.summary: A 28-year-old former organizer for Bernie Sanders won a major Democratic House election, defeating a man who had not been challenged in over a decade.

stories.headline: An Oil Giant Is Taking Big Steps. Saudi Arabia Can’t Afford for It to Slip.
stories.summary: Saudi Aramco, a major oil company, is taking big steps, but its planned public listing poses a risk to the company and the country.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders Is Winning Converts. But Primary Victories Remain Elusive.
stories.summary: Senator Sanders' ideas, such as a higher minimum wage and universal healthcare, are gaining popularity, but he and his allies are having trouble winning elections.

rounds.blurb: President Trump called for depriving immigrants of due process rights. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez defeated Joseph Crowley in a Democratic House election. Bernie Sanders' ideas gained popularity, influencing the political landscape.

stories.headline: WEST VIRGINIA ALSO WON BY ROOSEVELT; Colonel Carries 24 Counties, Which Gives Him a Majority of State Convention Delegates.
stories.summary: Roosevelt won in West Virginia, taking 24 counties and gaining a majority of state convention delegates. This was based on primary results from those counties.

stories.headline: THRILLING STORY BY TITANIC'S SURVIVING WIRELESS MAN; Bride Tells How He and Phillips Worked and How He Finished a Stoker Who Tried to Steal Phillips's Life Belt -- Ship Sank to Tune of "Autumn"
stories.summary: The Titanic sank while its band played the song 'Autumn', resulting in the deaths of 1,595 people, with 745 survivors rescued by the Carpathia. The ship's surviving wireless operator shared his account of the disaster, including the sending of distress messages.

stories.headline: SAIONJI'S REPLY ENDS MAGDALENA BUGABOO; Japanese Premier's Message to The Time, Washington Agrees, Closes the "Incident."
stories.summary: Japanese Premier Saionji's message has resolved an incident, with Washington considering the matter closed and preparing a response to a related resolution.

stories.headline: $100,000,000 PRICE ON RAILWAY MERGER; New Haven Must Spend This on Improvements, Says Foss, to Take Over Boston & Maine.
stories.summary: To take over the Boston & Maine railway, the New Haven railway must spend $100 million on improvements in and around Boston, according to Governor Foss.

rounds.blurb: The Titanic sank, resulting in 1,595 deaths and 745 survivors. The ship's band played "Autumn" as it went down. Roosevelt won West Virginia's primary, gaining a majority of state convention delegates.

stories.headline: POTSDAM TERMS ACCEPTED, DOMEI SAYS; An Imperial Message Is Forthcoming Soon,' Official News Agency Reports in Broadcast at 1:49 A.M. DISCLOSES LONG SESSION BY CABINET FCC Monitors Hear Code Dispatch Being Sent by Japanese to Switzerland--Washington Began to Suspect 'Stalling' Message to Switzerland Heard Cheering Greets Report in Guam Capital Awaited Reply All Day DOMEI ANNOUNCES FOE'S SURRENDER Japanese End Vigil Swiss Blame Japanese
stories.summary: Japan has accepted the terms of surrender, according to the official news agency, and an imperial message is expected soon. The announcement was made after a long cabinet session.

stories.headline: ARMED ESCORT SET; 350 Planes Will Carry Troops to an Airfield Southwest of Tokyo JAPAN REPORTS TYPHOON Says Storm in Kanto District Has Delayed Preparations for Entry of Allies Ships to Begin Disarmament Asks for Data on Newsmen Five Exceptions Requested 7,500 SKY TROOPS TO LAND IN JAPAN JAPAN WARNED ON CLASHES Home Ministry Issues Instructions to People During Occupation
stories.summary: Japan is preparing for the arrival of 7,500 Allied troops, who will be escorted by 350 planes, and has warned its citizens to avoid clashes with the occupying forces.

stories.headline: FRENCH ASK LOANS OF BILLION HERE; Paris Officials Reveal Request as de Gaulle and Party Return by Plane TOP PRIORITY ALSO SOUGHT France Wants Credits Given in Three Parts, as Well as Aid in Reconstruction Believe Air Is Cleared No Pledge on Third Loan
stories.summary: France has requested a loan of $1 billion from the US, as well as aid in reconstruction, but is reportedly disappointed with the US plan to distribute captured gold among liberated countries.

stories.headline: NEW DEPARTMENTS IN CABINET SOUGHT TO MERGE BUREAUS; Public Works, Veteran-Affairs, Public Enterprises Urged With Welfare as Entities FOR WIDE CONSOLIDATIONS Efficiency and Economy Would Result, Sponsors Say, Looking for Truman Backing Quicker Check-ups Expected Byrd Discussions Cited NEW DEPARTMENTS IN CABINET SOUGHT Exemptions Asked in Past
stories.summary: There is a proposal to create new departments in the US cabinet to merge and consolidate various government bureaus, with the goal of increasing efficiency and economy.

rounds.blurb: Japan accepted the terms of surrender, and an imperial message was forthcoming. Allied troops prepared to land in Japan, escorted by 350 planes. France requested a $1 billion loan from the US for reconstruction aid.
